Relatives of the deceased are obliged to be patient, practice self restraint, and gracefully accept Allaah’s decree because Allaah has already promised mankind that He would test them. } ِوَﻟَﻨَﺒْﻠُﻮَﻧﱠﻜُﻢْ ﺑِﺸَﻲْءٍ ﻣﱢﻦَ اﻟْﺨَﻮْفِ وَاﻟْﺠُﻮعِ وَﻧَﻘْﺺٍ ﻣﱢﻦَ اْﻷَﻣْﻮَال وَاْﻷَﻧْﻔُﺲِ وَاﻟﺜﱠﻤَﺮَاتِ وَﺑَﺸﱢﺮِ اﻟﺼﱠﺎﺑِﺮِﯾﻦَ { “Verily I21 will test you with some fear, hunger and a loss of wealth, life and the fruit (of your labor) so, give glad tidings to the patient ones.”22 Among the glad tidings for those who are patient when loved ones are lost is a home in paradise. Aboo Moosaa al-Ash‘aree quoted Allaah’s Messenger (r) as saying: ))ﻼﹶ ﻟِﻤﺩِ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪﺒ ﺍﻟﹾﻌﻟﹶﺩﺎﺕﹶ ﻭ ﺇِﺫﹶﺍ ﻤ ﺓﹶ ﺭ ﺜﹶﻤﺘﹸﻡﻀﻘﹸﻭلُ ﻗﹶﺒ ﻓﹶﻴﻡ ﻨﹶﻌﻘﹸﻭﻟﹸﻭﻥﺩِﻱ ﻓﹶﻴﺒ ﻋﻟﹶﺩ ﻭﺘﹸﻡﻀﺌِﻜﹶﺘِﻪِ ﻗﹶﺒ ﻨﹸﻭﺍ  ﺍﺒﻘﹸﻭلُ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪ ﻓﹶﻴﻊﺠﺘﹶﺭﺍﺴ ﻭﻙﻤِﺩ ﺤﻘﹸﻭﻟﹸﻭﻥﺩِﻱ ﻓﹶﻴﺒﺎﺫﹶﺍ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﻋﻘﹸﻭلُ ﻤ ﻓﹶﻴﻡ ﻨﹶﻌﻘﹸﻭﻟﹸﻭﻥﻓﹸﺅَﺍﺩِﻩِ ﻓﹶﻴ ﻴ ﺒﻭﻩﻤﺴﻨﱠﺔِ ﻭﺘﹰﺎ ﻓِﻲ ﺍﻟﹾﺠﻴﺩِﻱ ﺒﺒﻟِﻌ ﺩِﻤﺕﹶ ﺍﻟﹾﺤ ((. “If a child of [Allaah’s] slave dies, Allaah will say to His angels: You took My slave’s child. They will reply: Yes. He will then say: You took the love of his life23, and they will reply: Yes. He will then ask: What did My slave say? and they will reply: He praised you and said, ‘Surely, we belong to Allaah and to Him we will return.’ Allaah will then say: Build a house in paradise for My slave and call it the house of praise.”24 However, true patience for which Allaah’s promises reward is that which is practised when the calamity first strikes. The real test of patience is at the time of 21 Literally “we” known in English as the “majestic we” or the “royal we”, used by those of great importance. 22 surah al-Baqarah 2:155 23 Literally “the fruit of his heart”. 24 sahih Sunan at-Tirmithee, vol.1, pp.298-9, no.814.

the calamity. Being patient after the initial shock and reaction has worn off, is not considered real patience. َ ﻗﹶﺎلﻨﹾﻪ ﻋﻀِﻲ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪﺎﻟِﻙٍ ﺭﻥِ ﻤ ﺃَﻨﹶﺱِ ﺒﻥﻋ :  ﺍﻟﻨﱠﺒِﻲﺭﻤ r ﺭٍ ﻓﹶﻘﹶﺎلَ ﻗﹶﺒﻜِﻲ ﻋِﻨﹾﺩﺃَﺓٍ ﺘﹶﺒﺭ ﺒِﺎﻤ : )) ﺍِﺘﱠﻘِﻲ ﺒِﺭِﻱ ﺍﺼ ﻭﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪ (( ﻗﹶﺎﻟﹶﺕﹾ : ﺍﻟﻨﱠﺒِﺎ ﺇِﻨﱠﻪ ﻓﹶﻘِﻴلَ ﻟﹶﻬﺭِﻓﹾﻪ ﺘﹶﻌﻟﹶﻡﺘِﻲ ﻭﺼِﻴﺒ ﺒِﻤﺏ ﺘﹸﺼ ﻟﹶﻡﻨﱢﻲ ﻓﹶﺈِﻨﱠﻙ ﻋﻙﺇِﻟﹶﻴ ﻲ r  ﺍﻟﻨﱠﺒِﻲﺎﺏ ﻓﹶﺄَﺘﹶﺕﹾ ﺒ r ﻓﹶﻘﹶﺎﻟﹶﺕﹾﺍﺒِﻴﻥﻭ ﺒﻩ ﻋِﻨﹾﺩ ﺘﹶﺠِﺩ ﻓﹶﻠﹶﻡ : ﻓﹶﻘﹶﺎلَﺭِﻓﹾﻙ ﺃَﻋﻟﹶﻡ : ))  ﻋِﻨﹾﺩﺭﺒﺎ ﺍﻟﺼﺇِﻨﱠﻤ ﺔِ ﺍﹾﻷُﻭﻟﹶﻰﻤﺩﺍﻟﺼ ((. Anas ibn Maalik related that once the Prophet (r) passed by a woman crying beside a grave and said to her, “Fear Allaah and be patient.” She replied, “Get away from me, for you haven’t been afflicted with my misfortune. And you don’t even know what it is!” Someone informed her that it was the Prophet (r) [and the news struck her like death.]25 She then ran to the Prophet’s (r) door - where she did not find any guard - and called out, “O Messenger of Allaah, verily I didn’t know it was you.” The Prophet (r) replied, “Verily patience is with the first shock.”26 Patience and self restraint at the death of children have especially great rewards because of the severity of their death on their parents. Aboo Hurayrah reported that the Prophet (r) said, )) َﻠﹸﻐﹸﻭﺍ ﺍﻟﹾﺤِﻨﹾﺙﹶ ﺇِﻻﱠ ﺃﺒ ﻴﻻﹶﺩٍ ﻟﹶﻡﺎ ﺜﹶﻼﹶﺜﹶﺔﹸ ﺃَﻭﻤﻨﹶﻬﻴﻭﺕﹸ ﺒﻤﻥِ ﻴﻴﻠِﻤﺴ ﻤﺎ ﻤِﻥﻤ لِ  ﺒِﻔﹶﻀﺎ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪﻤﺨﹶﻠﹶﻬﺩ ﺨﹸﻠﹸﻭﺍ ﻘﹶﺎلُ ﺍﺩﺎﺅُﻨﹶﺎ ﻓﹶﻴﺨﹸلَ ﺁﺒﺩﺘﱠﻰ ﻴ ﺤﻘﹸﻭﻟﹸﻭﻥﻨﱠﺔﹶ ﻓﹶﻴﺨﹸﻠﹸﻭﺍ ﺍﻟﹾﺠ ﺍﺩﻡﻘﹶﺎلُ ﻟﹶﻬﻨﱠﺔﹶ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﻴ ﺍﻟﹾﺠﻡﺎﻫﺘِﻪِ ﺇِﻴﻤﺤﺭ ﺎﺅُﻜﹸﻡﺁﺒ ﻭﻨﱠﺔﹶ ﺃَﻨﹾﺘﹸﻡﺍﻟﹾﺠ ((. “Allaah will put any two Muslims who have three children that die, before reaching maturity, in paradise along with them due to His mercy. 27 The children 25 This addition is in the narration of Sahih Muslim. 26 Sahih Al-Bukhari vol.2, p.208, no. 372, Sahih Muslim, vol.2, p.439, no.2013, Sunan Abu Dawud, vol.2, p.890 no.3118, Sunan Ibn-i-Majah, vol.2, p.446 no.1596 and Mishkat AlMasabih,, vol.1, p.361. The wording is from Sunan Ibn-i-Majah, vol.2, p.452, no.1605. 27Sahih Al-Bukhari vol.2, pp.192-3, nos. 340-2, Sahih Muslim, vol.4, pp.1384-5, nos.6365 & 6368.

will wait at the gates of paradise. When they are told to enter, they will reply, ‘Not until our two parents come.’ They will be told to enter paradise along with their parents by Allaah’s mercy.” 28 ِﻭلِ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪﺴ ﺭﻥﺜِﻲ ﻋﺩﺤﺎ ﺃَﻨﹾﺕﹶ ﻤﻨﹶﺎﻥِ ﻓﹶﻤ ﺍﺒﺎﺕﹶ ﻟِﻲ ﻤ ﻗﹶﺩﺓﹶ ﺇِﻨﱠﻪﺭﻴﺭ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﻗﹸﻠﹾﺕﹸ ِﻷَﺒِﻲ ﻫﺎﻥﺴ ﺃَﺒِﻲ ﺤﻥﻋ rﻡﺘﹶﺎﻨﹶﺎ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﻨﹶﻌﻭ ﻤﻥﻨﹶﺎ ﻋ ﺒِﻪِ ﺃَﻨﹾﻔﹸﺴﺏﺩِﻴﺙٍ ﺘﹸﻁﹶﻴ ﺒِﺤ :)) ﺘﹶﻠﹶﻘﱠﻰ ﻨﱠﺔِ ﻴ ﺍﻟﹾﺠﺎﻤِﻴﺹﻋ ﺩﻡﻫﺼِﻐﹶﺎﺭ ﺃَﺤ ﻰ ﺘﹶﻨﹶﺎﻫﺫﹶﺍ ﻓﹶﻼﹶ ﻴ ﻫﺒِﻙﻨِﻔﹶﺔِ ﺜﹶﻭﺎ ﺁﺨﹸﺫﹸ ﺃَﻨﹶﺎ ﺒِﺼﺩِﻩِ ﻜﹶﻤ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﺒِﻴﺒِﻪِ ﺃَﻭﺄْﺨﹸﺫﹸ ﺒِﺜﹶﻭﻪِ ﻓﹶﻴﻴﻭ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﺃَﺒ ﺃَﻭﺎﻩ ﺃَﺒﻡﻫﺩ ﻨﱠﺔﹶ  ﺍﻟﹾﺠﺎﻩﺃَﺒ ﻭ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪﺨِﻠﹶﻪﺩﺘﱠﻰ ﻴﻨﹾﺘﹶﻬِﻲ ﺤ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﻓﹶﻼﹶ ﻴﺃَﻭ ((. Aboo Hassaan reported: “I said to Aboo Hurayrah that my two children had died. Would you narrate to me anything from Allaah’s Messenger (r) a hadith which would soothe our hearts in our bereavements ? He said: Yes. Small children are the fowls of paradise. If one of them meets his father,29 he would grab his garment,30 and he will not remove his hand from it until Allaah causes his father to enter paradise.”31 Allaah has promised a special additional reward for women who lose three children. Because she suffers even more than her husband when a child dies. It is she who carries the child within her for nine months, gives birth to him, nurses him, etc. If she is patient, she will be granted an even greater reward than her spouse. ِﻭلَ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪﺴ ﺭﺓﹶ ﺃَﻥﺭﻴﺭ ﺃَﺒِﻲ ﻫﻥﻋ r ﺎﺭِ ﺍﹾﻷَﻨﹾﺼﺓٍ ﻤِﻥﻭ ﻗﹶﺎلَ ﻟِﻨِﺴ : ))  ﺜﹶﻼﹶﺜﹶﺔﹲ ﻤِﻥﺍﻜﹸﻥﺩﻭﺕﹸ ِﻹِﺤﻤ ﻻﹶ ﻴ ﻨﱠﺔﹶﺨﹶﻠﹶﺕِ ﺍﻟﹾﺠ ﺇِﻻﱠ ﺩﻪﺘﹶﺴِﺒﻟﹶﺩِ ﻓﹶﺘﹶﺤﺍﻟﹾﻭ (( ﻥﺃَﺓﹲ ﻤِﻨﹾﻬﺭﻓﹶﻘﹶﺎﻟﹶﺕِ ﺍﻤ : ﻭلَ ﺍﻟﻠﱠﻪِ؟ ﻗﹶﺎلَﺴﺎ ﺭﻥِ ﻴﺃَﻭِ ﺍﺜﹾﻨﹶﻴ ) : ) ﺃَﻭِ ﻥِﺍﺜﹾﻨﹶﻴ ((. Aboo Hurayrah quoted the Messenger of Allaah (r) as saying to a group of Ansaaree women, “Any woman who has three children that die will be shielded 28 sahih Sunan an-Nasaa’ee vol.2, p.405 no.1770. 29 The narrator added, “or he demonstrated with his hand saying: the way I take hold of the hem of your garment.” 30 The narrator added, “or he said: his parents”. 31 Sahih Muslim, vol.4, p.1385 no.6370.

by them from the Hellfire.” When a woman asked about two children, he replied that would also shield her.32
content_id:
Keluarga almarhum wajib bersabar, mengendalikan diri, dan menerima ketetapan Allah dengan lapang dada, karena Allah telah berjanji kepada manusia bahwa Dia akan menguji mereka: "Dan sungguh akan Kami berikan cobaan kepadamu dengan sedikit ketakutan, kelaparan, kekurangan harta, jiwa dan buah-buahan. Dan berikanlah kabar gembira kepada orang-orang yang sabar." Di antara kabar gembira bagi mereka yang bersabar ketika kehilangan orang-orang yang dicintai adalah sebuah rumah di surga. Abu Musa al-Asy'ari mengutip Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, "Jika anak seorang hamba meninggal, Allah berfirman kepada para malaikat-Nya: Kalian telah mengambil anak hamba-Ku. Mereka menjawab: Ya. Dia kemudian bertanya: Kalian telah mengambil buah hatinya, dan mereka menjawab: Ya. Dia kemudian bertanya: Apa yang diucapkan hamba-Ku? dan mereka menjawab: Ia memuji-Mu dan mengucapkan 'Sesungguhnya kami milik Allah dan kepada-Nya kami kembali.' Allah kemudian berfirman: Bangunkan sebuah rumah di surga untuk hamba-Ku dan namakan rumah itu dengan rumah pujian."

Namun, kesabaran sejati yang dijanjikan Allah pahalanya adalah yang dipraktikkan saat musibah pertama kali menimpa. Anas bin Malik menceritakan bahwa suatu kali Nabi ﷺ melewati seorang wanita yang sedang menangis di samping sebuah kuburan dan berkata kepadanya, "Bertakwalah kepada Allah dan bersabarlah." Wanita itu menjawab, "Pergilah dariku, karena engkau tidak tertimpa musibah seperti yang aku alami. Dan engkau bahkan tidak tahu apa itu!" Seseorang memberitahunya bahwa itu adalah Nabi ﷺ. Wanita itu kemudian berlari ke pintu Nabi ﷺ — tempat tidak ada penjaga — dan berseru, "Wahai Rasulullah, sesungguhnya aku tidak tahu itu adalah engkau." Nabi ﷺ menjawab, "Sesungguhnya kesabaran itu pada guncangan pertama."

Allah telah menjanjikan pahala tambahan yang istimewa bagi wanita yang kehilangan tiga anak. Karena wanita menanggung beban yang lebih besar dari suaminya ketika seorang anak meninggal. Abu Hurairah mengutip Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepada sekelompok wanita Anshar, "Wanita mana pun yang memiliki tiga anak yang meninggal, mereka akan menjadi tameng baginya dari neraka." Ketika seorang wanita bertanya tentang dua anak, beliau menjawab bahwa hal itu pun akan menjadi tameng baginya.
content_ar:
الصبر على المصيبة فريضة دينية وخلق إسلامي رفيع. وقد أثنى القرآن الكريم على الصابرين ثناءً بالغاً: ﴿وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ، الَّذِينَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُمْ مُصِيبَةٌ قَالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ، أُولَئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَوَاتٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ وَأُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْمُهْتَدُونَ﴾ (البقرة: 155-157).

مراتب الصبر

أعلى مراتب الصبر ما كان عند الصدمة الأولى — كما في الحديث النبوي «الصبر عند الصدمة الأولى». ويُراد بالصبر هنا حبس النفس عن السخط والنياحة والاعتراض على القدر، لا غياب الحزن من القلب فإنَّ الحزن طبيعي ومشروع.

الأجر الموعود على الصبر

بشَّر النبي ﷺ من يصبر على موت ولده بالجنة. وفي رواية أنَّ الله يقول للملائكة حين يقبض ولد عبده: «قبضتم ولد عبدي؟» فيقولون: نعم، فيقول: «ماذا قال عبدي؟» فيقولون: حمدك واسترجع، فيقول الله: «ابنوا له بيتاً في الجنة وسمُّوه بيت الحمد».
